'''Mesa Greenbark''' was a [[Gender/Legends|female]] [[Nosaurian/Legends|Nosaurian]] who married [[Bomo Greenbark]], a [[Commander/Legends|commander]] of the [[New Plympto resistance (Imperial Period)|New Plympto resistance]] who fought against the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]. With Bomo, she had had a daughter who they named [[Resa Greenbark|Resa]] and after the declaration of the [[New Order/Legends|New Order]], the political regime of the Empire, she and her child attempted to evacuate their [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] to escape Imperial forces. While her husband and the rest of the resistance fought one final battle at [[Half-Axe Pass]], Mesa and Resa attempted to reach a [[Spaceport/Legends|spaceport]] in an area called [[Cadgel Meadows]], but were captured by Imperials before reaching their destination. The evacuees were [[Slavery/Legends|enslaved]] and taken to the planet [[Orvax IV]] where Mesa was [[Death/Legends|killed]] attempting to prevent her daughter being sold.

==Biography==
Mesa Greenbark lived on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[New Plympto/Legends|New Plympto]] in the [[Corellian sector/Legends|Corellian sector]] of the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]], the [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] of her [[Species/Legends|species]]. Mesa married another Nosaurian named [[Bomo Greenbark]] and with him had a female child, who they named [[Resa Greenbark|Resa]]. When the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]] began between the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] and the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]], the Nosaurians came to side with the Confederacy, and fought against Republic forces on their [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]]. After the defeat of the Confederacy in [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]], the [[New Plympto resistance (Imperial Period)|New Plympto resistance]] was left as one of the [[Separatist holdouts/Legends|Separatist holdouts]] and continued to fight against the newly formed [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] in a conflict that became known as the [[Cleansing of New Plympto]].

A [[Standard month/Legends|month]] after the declaration of the [[New Order/Legends|New Order]], the [[Politics/Legends|political]] regime of the Empire, the Nosaurians begun evacuating their women and children from New Plympto including Resa and Mesa. While the other male Nosaurians continued fighting the Empire in an area known as [[Half-Axe Pass]], Bomo personally saw his family and other evacuees to the transports they were using to escape. Before boarding the transport, Mesa and Resa asked Bomo to leave with them, but when he refused, they said a final farewell and boarded the final vehicle available. With the final passengers aboard the transports, the journey to the [[Spaceport/Legends|spaceport]] in the area known as [[Cadgel Meadows]] began, where Mesa and Resa intended to find transport to the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Sullust/Legends|Sullust]] in the [[Brema sector/Legends|Brema sector]] of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]].

The transports never reached Cadgel Meadows and were instead stopped by Imperial forces. All Nosaurians on board, including Mesa and her daughter, were then captured and moved off world to be sold as slaves. The Imperials brought them to the world of [[Orvax IV]], a well known slave world, where they hoped to sell them to the highest bidder. When the slavers came to take Resa for sale however Mesa attempted to prevent her daughter being separated from her and fought the slavers. Her attempts were in vain however and Mesa was killed in the attempt to save her daughter.

===Legacy===
With Mesa's attempt to save her a failure, Resa was taken and sold by a [[Chagrian/Legends|Chagrian]] slaver named [[Orso Meeto]] to the [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Dezono Qua]]. A day after Mesa's death, her husband arrived to save her and became distraught when he discovered he was too late. Bomo then left to find Resa but once again failed to save his family as Qua had had her cooked and consumed her, an act for which Bomo's friend [[Dass Jennir]], a [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]], killed him.

==Behind the scenes==
Mesa Greenbark appeared in the ''[[Star Wars: Dark Times]]'' [[Star Wars comic books|comic book]] series.
